scope.target: Durable predecessor ordering and compact backlog roadmap promotion
constraints: Do not rewrite the final assessment report or finding conclusions
Keep task-level execution progress in child workflows
Make the backlog roadmap the compact discovery entry

steps: Commit and index ASM-20260717-004 on its dedicated assessment branch
Rebase the unpushed planning commit onto the assessment commit
Create .dev/backlog/ROADMAP.md with release-level state and activation gates
Move the independently authored Fable 5 plan to .dev/backlog/plans/ and record the historical assessment provenance addendum
Add reciprocal assessment, backlog, and workflow references
Validate assessment, backlog, workflow, context, and Git history
execution.last_completed_step: Promoted the compact roadmap into .dev/backlog/ROADMAP.md, corrected the historical assessment provenance, moved the independent plan to .dev/backlog/plans/, and repaired durable predecessor ordering and references.
execution.next_action: Read .dev/backlog/ROADMAP.md and confirm the exact subject commit for successor assessment formalization.

results.summary: ASM-20260717-004 now precedes the planning workflow in Git history as a historical assessment, the independent Fable 5 plan is retained under .dev/backlog/plans/, and .dev/backlog/ROADMAP.md is the compact cross-session roadmap source while workflow details remain the decision record.
tests_run: Assessment artifact validation passed for 7 assessments
Workflow artifact validation passed for 19 post-adoption workflows, 39 indexed workflow directories, and 11 backlog items
All six task JSON files parsed
results.residual_risk: The independent v0.4.0 planning source still lacks an exact immutable subject commit; the roadmap gates remediation on a governed successor assessment rather than an automatic full re-audit.
